methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 2 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Nano Nuclear Energy

data.narrative:
Oklo is a public-stage nuclear energy company founded by Jacob DeWitte and Caroline Cochran, currently valued at $10B+ after raising $500M+. The nuclear energy sector comprises a fragmented peer universe of 3 public companies and 8 private entities, including notable private peers such as TerraPower, Kairos Power, X-Energy, Nano Nuclear Energy, and Zap Energy.

The peer-median approach is not applicable to this valuation. The nuclear energy sector contains only 7 same-sector peers total, but filtering by stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier yields fewer than 3 datapoints in any narrow band—the minimum threshold for reliable triangulation. Applying an all-sector median would introduce substantial category error given the sector-specific capital intensity, regulatory burden, and development timelines that differentiate nuclear from broader energy comps. This constraint eliminates the primary quantitative anchor from the analysis.

Without sufficient narrow-peer density to support a triangulation methodology, the valuation cannot be supported by the data provided. A credible range would require either (1) expansion of the analysis to include broader energy or infrastructure peers at the cost of reduced comparability, or (2) procurement of additional transaction or financing data from the private peer set. The key uncertainty is whether Oklo's public-market valuation reflects genuine differentiation in technology or capital efficiency, or represents broader sector sentiment toward advanced nuclear that may not persist.
